title: artanthe granulosa
intro: Artanthe granulosa, also known as the "Sandalwood Creeper" or "Kulu oil creeper", is a tropical vine native to the Western Ghats region of India. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Artanthe granulosa include its ability to treat skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, as well as its potential to reduce stress and anxiety. Artanthe granulosa is typically used in herbal preparations, including essential oils and extracts, which can be applied topically or consumed in supplement form.
